articles.title: The Other Side Of The Partner Pay Gap — See Also
articles.content:
**年中审查即将到来**：是时候让一些合伙人大力投入或面临减薪了。

**人工智能速记员失误差点让嫌疑人获得律师**：他差点行使了获得律师代理的权利，结果却差之千里。

**律师因人工智能错误交回执照**：令人印象深刻的是，他似乎对此完全不以为意。

**盛德律师事务所将真金白银投入公益法律服务**：最多200小时的公益工作将计入合伙人的奖金工时！

这篇文章《合伙人薪酬差距的另一面——另见》首次发布于Above the Law法律资讯网。
articles.summary:
Mid-Year Check Ins Are Coming: Time for some partners to double down or expect a pay cut.

Mistaken AI Stenographer Almost Got A Suspect An Attorney: He came close to invoking the right to an attorney, yet landed so far.

Attorney Turns In License After AI Errors: Kind of impressive how little he seemed to care about it all.

Quinn Emmanuel Puts Their Money Where Their Pro-Bono Is: Up to 200 hours of pro bono work will count toward associates’ bonus hour totals!

The post The Other Side Of The Partner Pay Gap — See Also appeared first on Above the Law.

articles.title: Federal Judge’s Fitness Journey Is Everyone’s Problem
articles.content:
**编者按：** 欢迎来到我们的每日专栏“每日 trivia 问答”！

**问题：** 哪位保守派联邦法官在特朗普第二次就职当天开设了一个关于其“健康与养生之旅”的 Substack 专栏，详细记录了他们的饮食、睡眠和锻炼计划？

**提示：** 特朗普的司法提名者普遍偏年轻，如果没有重大改革，这可能会使他对最高法院的影响力延续数代人。这位法官写道：“我大部分血液检查结果现在都达到了43岁人群的理想范围。因此，我将我的生物年龄降低到了43岁（你的实际年龄其实并不重要，重要的是你的生物年龄）。” 据推测，这些言论是为了让这位出生于1969年的法官在谋求晋升至最高法院时更具吸引力。

这篇文章首发于 **Above the Law**。
articles.summary:
Ed. Note: Welcome to our daily feature Trivia Question of the Day!

Which conservative federal judge launched a substack about their “Health and Wellness Journey” (on the day of Donald Trump’s second inauguration) that details their diet, sleep, and workout regime?

Hint: Trump’s judicial picks skew on the younger side, which, absent major reform, will likely result in his dead hand influence on the Court for generations. This judge wrote, “Most of my bloodwork is now in the ideal range for a 43 year old!. As a result, I reduced my biological age to 43 (your chronological age really doesn’t matter; what matters is your biological age).” It’s speculated the comments are designed to make the judge, born in 1969, more appealing a candidate for elevation to Donald Trump.

The post Federal Judge’s Fitness Journey Is Everyone’s Problem appeared first on Above the Law.

articles.title: Attorney Turns In License After Lazy AI Lawyering
articles.content:
“律师因使用AI被抓”的新闻通常都带着一种“他们怎么这么蠢？！”的嘲笑意味，但这个故事更像是没人张罗欢送派对的意外退休。执业数十年后，律师以天鹅之歌收场并不罕见。既然要退场，何不来个辉煌的结局为职业生涯画上句号？然而，这位来自内布拉斯加的律师的最后一幕更像是一只鸽子的咕咕声。

据1011 Now报道：根据6月18日提交的法院意见，律师詹姆斯·汉密尔顿在面临纪律处分申诉时交出了执照。

申诉指控汉密尔顿在代理客户时做出虚假陈述，伪造信函和司法裁决。申诉还指控他的行为违反了作为内布拉斯加执业律师的宣誓誓言，违反了《内布拉斯加职业行为规则》的多项规定。

面对申诉，汉密尔顿交出了执照，表示他不会对这些指控的真实性提出异议。他还放弃了通知、出庭或听证的权利。

必须承认：这是我见过的最不惊天动地的AI幻觉后果。并不是说我们应该为把专业工作外包给LLM的行为鼓掌——我们之前就批评过史蒂文·费尔德曼的拙劣作品——甚至法官都忍不住凑热闹——但汉密尔顿对任何努力表现的漠视确实引人注目。真正的问题是，他是否有足够的精力像海森堡那样耸耸肩：

这次处分本可以是一封邮件。

祝你好运，希望你的未来事业需要更多的努力。

林肯律师因涉嫌伪造法律文件被取消执业资格 [1011 Now]

克里斯·威廉姆斯于2021年6月成为Above the Law的社交媒体经理和副编辑。在加入团队之前，他在Facebook群组“法学院梗图”中担任小有名气的梗图创作者。他在密苏里州熬到从华盛顿大学圣路易斯法学院毕业。他曾是造船工人，现在正在学游泳，对修辞学、斯宾诺莎主义和幽默感兴趣。重新开始骑自行车也无妨。可以通过christopherrashadwilliams@gmail.com或@WritesForRent联系他。

这篇文章《律师因懒惰使用AI交出执照》首次发表于Above the Law。
articles.summary:
“Lawyer Gets Caught Using AI” stories usually have a point and laugh, “How could they be so stupid?!” vibe to them, but this one feels more like a an unexpected retirement nobody bothered to plan a party for. After decades of lawyering, it isn’t uncommon for lawyers to go out on a swan song. And why not? If you’re gonna go out, do so in a blaze of glory that capstones your commitment to the craft! Far from it, this Nebraskan attorney’s final act was more like a pigeon coo.

From 1011 Now:

According to a court opinion filed June 18, attorney James Hamilton surrendered his license while facing a disciplinary complaint stemming from his representation of a client.

The complaint alleged Hamilton made false statements and fabricated correspondence and judicial decisions while representing the client. It also alleged that, by his actions, Hamilton violated his oath of office as an attorney licensed to practice law in Nebraska and violated several provisions of the Nebraska Rules of Professional Conduct.

In response to the complaint, Hamilton surrendered his license, stating he did not challenge or contest the truth of the allegations. He also waived his right to notice, appearance or a hearing.

Gotta give credit where credit is due: this is the hardest not with a bang but a whimper AI hallucination consequence I’ve ever seen. Not that we’d applaud making a big deal out of getting caught outsourcing your expertise to an LLM, we gave Steven Feldman a couple raps on the knuckles for his purple prose — hell, even a judge couldn’t resist getting in on the fun — but Hamiltion’s disdain for anything giving the appearance of effort is noteworthy. The real question is if he mustered up enough energy for a shoulder shrug a la Heisenberg:

This discipline could have been an e-mail.

Best of luck in your future endeavors. I hope they require more effort.

Lincoln Attorney Disbarred After Allegedly Fabricating Legal Documents [1011 Now]

Chris Williams became a social media manager and assistant editor for Above the Law in June 2021. Prior to joining the staff, he moonlighted as a minor Memelord™ in the Facebook group Law School Memes for Edgy T14s .  He endured Missouri long enough to graduate from Washington University in St. Louis School of Law. He is a former boat builder who is learning to swim and is interested in rhetoric, Spinozists and humor. Getting back in to cycling wouldn’t hurt either. You can reach him by email at christopherrashadwilliams@gmail.com and by Tweet/Bluesky at @WritesForRent.

The post Attorney Turns In License After Lazy AI Lawyering appeared first on Above the Law.

articles.title: Trump’s Persecution Of His Political Enemies Takes A Hit
articles.content:
**特朗普对其政敌的迫害遭遇挫折**

美国司法部正在对总统的政治对手展开刑事调查。此案只是其中一个例证，但我们每天都能看到本届政府无视法律的种种表现——无论是在明尼苏达州还是全国各地。我们所有人都必须继续寻求正义，维护法治。

——明尼苏达州州长蒂姆·沃尔兹，就美国地区法官帕特里克·席尔茨驳回特朗普政府传票请求的裁决发表上述评论。该传票针对沃尔兹、州检察长达基·埃里森、明尼阿波利斯市长雅各布·弗雷伊、圣保罗市长考伊·赫尔以及拉姆西县和亨内平县官员发出，指控这些明尼苏达州政治人物鼓励抗议者干扰移民和海关执法局的活动。席尔茨法官认定，传票的"主要目的"是"迫使明尼苏达州官员协助联邦政府执行民事移民法，并对他们未能这样做进行骚扰和报复"。

原文首发于法律资讯网站Above the Law。
articles.summary:
The U.S. Justice Department is pursuing criminal investigations into the President’s political opponents. This case was just one example of that, but we are seeing daily reminders of this administration’s lawlessness — in Minnesota and around the country. We all must continue to seek justice and uphold the rule of law.

—Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz, commenting on a ruling by  U.S. District Judge Patrick Schiltz blocking the Trump administration’s attempt to subpoena Walz, Attorney General Keith Ellison, Minneapolis Mayor Jacob Frey, St. Paul Mayor Kaohly Her and officials in Ramsey and Hennepin counties over allegations the Minnesotan politicos were encouraging protestors to disrupt Immigration and Customs Enforcement activity. Judge Schiltz found the “dominant purpose” of the subpoenas was to “coerce Minnesota officials into assisting the federal government with enforcing civil immigration law and to harass and retaliate against them for failing to do so.”

The post Trump’s Persecution Of His Political Enemies Takes A Hit appeared first on Above the Law.

articles.title: Legal Ethics Roundup: When Lawyers Protest, SCOTX Says Self-Rep Lawyer Can Contact Opposing Party, And More!
articles.content:
编者按：欢迎Renee Knake Jefferson重返Above the Law。请在此订阅她的Substack专栏Legal Ethics Roundup。

欢迎来到每周吸引、困扰、激励并令我惊喜的法律伦理世界。

周一快乐！

上周我忙于教学和发表文章。

今年夏天我教授两门课程——必修的高年级法学院课程“职业责任”以及写作研讨会“领导力、法律与权力”。

我继续与报道联邦司法纪律的记者交流，本周在彭博法律（Bloomberg Law）文章《法官不当行为案件引发对紧张法院的额外审查》中被引用。以下是简要摘录：

最近的行为也引发了更广泛的问题：现有旨在追究联邦法官责任的制度是否有效，因为在日益政治化的环境中，法官面临更严格的审查，休斯顿大学司法伦理专家Renee Knake Jefferson表示。“危险不仅在于对个别法官的声誉损害，更在于对司法机构作为制度的公众信心的侵蚀，”Jefferson补充道。……Jefferson表示，严重的个人不当行为并不总是涉及法官在法庭上的行为，但司法系统已承认这些无关的不当行为可能影响法院。“问题通常不在于该行为是否影响了特定案件，而在于它是否破坏了公众对法官履行职务标准的能力的信心，”她说。

在此阅读更多。

我在517商业杂志上发表了一篇关于解决未满足法律需求和恢复公众对司法系统信任的专栏文章。以下是摘录，您可以在这里阅读全文。

最后，我的《密歇根法律评论》文章《当律师抗议时》已作为年度书评期刊发表。以下是摘要：

在此免费下载完整评论。

当律师走上街头时会发生什么？2025年5月，美国各地超过10,000名律师参加了协调一致的“法律日”示威活动，捍卫法治、司法独立和宪法治理。然而，就在四年前，律师却在导致1月6日美国国会大厦袭击事件的活动中发挥了突出作用。这两个截然不同的事件揭示了法律伦理中一个基本但 largely unexplored的问题：律师 activism何时加强法治，何时削弱法治？

本评论使用Ray Brescia的《律师国家》和W. Bradley Wendel的《取消律师》两本新书作为 lens，通过它们来审视参与抗议和其他形式公共 activism 的律师的伦理义务。尽管两本书都不是直接关注抗议，但都阐明了法律职业与民主、问责、异议和法治的关系。通过借鉴他们的历史叙述和当代案例研究，本评论认为律师抗议揭示了职业监管中的一个重大差距。虽然律师被指示作为法律系统的官员和公共公民维护法治，但现有的职业行为规则几乎没有提供关于律师如何在客户代理之外进行 activism 的实际指导。

本评论通过从民权运动、气候 activism 到1月6日和当代法治示威活动的案例研究，构建了一个理解律师抗议的框架。它认为法律伦理既不应该阻止律师参与民主抗议，也不应该完全放任这种参与不受监管。相反，职业规范应该培养推进民主价值观、问责制和宪法治理的抗议，同时为威胁法律机构和法治本身的行为建立更清晰的保障措施。在缺乏正式改革的情况下，法学院、律师协会和非营利组织可以在帮助律师履行这些责任方面发挥重要作用。

上周亮点——十大头条新闻

#1“律师走上街头捍卫法治。”来自纽约州律师协会：“周二，律师们聚集在纽约市 Foley Square，重新承诺捍卫法治。集会的主题是‘是时候聘请律师了’，演讲者鼓励律师站出来，承担更多公益工作，为移民和联邦工人辩护，保护投票权。”在此阅读更多。

#2“法官驳回Hannah Dugan推翻ICE案件陪审团裁定的请求。”来自密尔沃基哨兵报：“一名联邦法官驳回了前密尔沃基县巡回法官Hannah Dugan推翻陪审团裁定的请求，该陪审团裁定她在2025年4月于法庭外阻碍ICE特工，此案将重新进入量刑阶段。”在此阅读更多。

#3“CLEAR报告对法律职业监管意味着什么。”来自首席大法官Megan K. Cavanah在密歇根律师杂志：“如果你能'像律师一样思考'，这是否意味着你能'像律师一样行事'？这是去年由法律教育和招生改革委员会（CLEAR报告）发布的一份报告中提出并回答的问题。该报告得到了首席法官会议（CCJ）和州法院行政会议（COSCA）的认可，反映了广泛的利益相关者分析，包括来自数千名法官、律师和法学院学生的反馈。CLEAR发现了什么？”在此阅读更多。

#4“法官在乔治亚州选民名单案件中回避。”来自Politico：“亚特兰大一名陷入困境的联邦法官同意了司法部的要求，从寻求获取乔治亚州选民名单的联邦诉讼中退出。周一发布、周二公布的命令中，美国地区法官Eleanor Ross表示，她因此参加富尔顿县地方检察官Fani Willis的2024年政治活动而回避此案，Willis对总统唐纳德·特朗普干涉2020年总统选举提起刑事诉讼。”在此阅读更多。

#5“德克萨斯州律师在对手无接触规则案件中胜诉。”来自彭博法律：“德克萨斯州最高法院周五裁定，该州的纪律规则并未明确禁止代表自己的律师联系他知道由律师代理的对方当事人。法官们暗示可能即将进行行政变更以禁止此类沟通，并表示他们面前的案件不是进行此类变更的适当场所。'我们不会通过意见来修改规则，'Justice Debra Lehrmann为 nearly unanimous 法院写道。法院对无接触规则的解释推翻了一审法院对持证律师William Ruth处以的五年停职处分，该处分已被上诉法院维持。”在此阅读更多。

#6“德克萨斯大学法学院修改AI政策以阻止技能流失。”来自彭博法律：“德克萨斯大学法学院院长呼吁对生成式人工智能政策采取新方法，以应对这项技术侵蚀基本技能学习的风险。教师应在教学中强调苏格拉底式提问，法学院院长Robert 'Bobby' Chesney在周二给员工的备忘录中表示。他们应该将课堂时间视为'教授可以确定学生正在学习而非使用AI的唯一场所'。”在此阅读更多。

#7“SCOTUSblog创始人Goldstein请求无罪或重审被驳回。”来自Law360：“马里兰州一名联邦法官周二驳回了SCOTUSblog创始人Tom Goldstein请求无罪或重审的请求，驳回了他关于陪审团指示和排除证据问题需要重审的主张，这些问题出现在他的逃税和抵押贷款欺诈案件中。”在此阅读更多。

#8“最高法院不会审理98岁停职法官保留职位的请求。”来自今日美国：“美国最高法院于6月15日拒绝审理该国最年长的联邦法官、98岁的Pauline Newman的请求，她试图推翻2023年因 fitness to serve 调查而被停职的决定。”在此阅读更多。

#9“AI能力不再是可选项，法官告诉律师大会。”来自佛罗里达律师新闻：“资深法官在6月17日佛罗里达律师大会 Orlando 举行的研讨会上警告称，佛罗里达律师有责任熟悉人工智能，这项正在改变法律实践和法庭程序规则的技术。'我们都有基本的胜任能力要求，每个人在这方面都必须做的至少是了解这些工具如何运作的基本概念，'美国治安法官Nicholas Mizell说，他在佛罗里达州南部地区Ft. Myers主持工作。”在此阅读更多。

#10“前特朗普律师要求撤销受谴责法官的1月6裁决。”来自彭博法律：“前特朗普白宫律师Stefan Passantino要求亚特兰大一名被谴责的联邦审判法官撤销之前对他不利的裁决，该裁决涉及一项挑战美国众议院1月6日特别委员会的诉讼，理由是法官与民主党人Fani Willis关系密切。美国北区乔治亚州地区法院法官Eleanor Ross此前已承认参加了Willis的竞选胜利派对，并从另一涉及美国司法部试图获取乔治亚州选民名单的政治案件中回避，Passantino周四的动议如是说。”在此阅读更多。

保持联系

Renee Knake Jefferson持有Doherty法律伦理捐赠主席席位，是休斯顿大学法学院教授。在Legal Ethics Roundup查看更多她的文章。在X（前Twitter）上关注她@reneeknake，或在Bluesky上关注legalethics.bsky.social。

这篇文章首次出现在Above the Law。
articles.summary:
Ed. note: Please welcome Renee Knake Jefferson back to the pages of Above the Law. Subscribe to her Substack, Legal Ethics Roundup, here.

Welcome to what captivates, haunts, inspires, and surprises me every week in the world of legal ethics.

Happy Monday!

Last week was a flurry of teaching and publishing.

I’m teaching two classes this summer – the mandatory upper-level law school class “Professional Responsibility” and a writing seminar “Leadership, Law & Power.”

I continued to engage with journalists covering discipline in the federal judiciary, this week quoted in the Bloomberg Law article, “Judges’ Misconduct Cases Bring Extra Scrutiny to Strained Courts.” Here’s a brief excerpt:

The recent conduct also raises broader questions on whether the existing systems set up to hold federal judges accountable are effective, as judges face heightened scrutiny in an increasingly politicized environment, said Renee Knake Jefferson, a University of Houston judicial ethics expert. “The danger is not merely reputational harm to those individual judges but an erosion of public confidence in the judiciary as an institution,” Jefferson added. … Jefferson said serious personal misconduct doesn’t always implicate a judge’s behavior on the bench, but that the judiciary has recognized that those unrelated misdeeds can impact the courts. “The question is often not whether the conduct affected a particular case, but whether it undermines confidence in the judge’s ability to uphold the standards of the office,” she said.

Read more here.

My op-ed on solving unmet legal needs and restoring public trust in the justice system was published in the 517 Business Magazine. An excerpt is featured below and you can read it in full here.

Finally, my Michigan Law Review piece “When Lawyers Protest” was published as part of the annual book review issue. Here’s the abstract:

Download the full Review for free here.

What happens when lawyers take to the streets? In May 2025, more than 10,000 lawyers across the United States participated in coordinated ‘Law Day’ demonstrations to defend the rule of law, judicial independence, and constitutional governance. Just four years earlier, however, lawyers played prominent roles in efforts that culminated in the January 6 attack on the U.S. Capitol. These contrasting events expose a fundamental but largely unexplored question in legal ethics: When does lawyer activism strengthen the rule of law, and when does it undermine it?

This Review uses two recent books, Lawyer Nation by Ray Brescia and Canceling Lawyers by W. Bradley Wendel, as a lens through which to examine the ethical obligations of lawyers who engage in protest and other forms of public activism. Although neither book focuses directly on protest, both illuminate the legal profession’s relationship to democracy, accountability, dissent, and the rule of law. Drawing on their historical accounts and contemporary case studies, this Review argues that lawyer protest reveals a significant gap in professional regulation. While lawyers are instructed to uphold the rule of law as officers of the legal system and public citizens, existing professional conduct rules provide little practical guidance about how lawyers should navigate activism undertaken outside the context of client representation.

The Review develops a framework for understanding lawyer protest through case studies ranging from the civil rights movement and climate activism to January 6 and contemporary rule-of-law demonstrations. It argues that legal ethics should neither discourage lawyer participation in democratic protest nor leave such participation entirely unregulated. Instead, professional norms should cultivate protest that advances democratic values, accountability, and constitutional governance while establishing clearer safeguards against conduct that threatens legal institutions and the rule of law itself. In the absence of formal reform, law schools, bar organizations, and nonprofit institutions can play an important role in preparing lawyers to meet these responsibilities.

Highlights from Last Week – Top Ten Headlines

#1 “Lawyers Take to the Streets To Defend the Rule of Law.” From the New York State Bar Association: “Lawyers filled Foley Square in New York City Tuesday to rededicate themselves to defending the rule of law. The theme of the rally was ‘Time to Lawyer Up,’ with speakers encouraging lawyers to step up and take on more pro-bono work defending immigrants and federal workers and protecting voting rights.” Read more here.

#2 “Judge Denies Hannah Dugan’s Bid to Overturn Jury Verdict in ICE Case.” From the Milwaukee Journal Sentinel: “A federal judge has denied former Milwaukee County Circuit Judge Hannah Dugan’s bid to overturn a jury’s verdict that she obstructed ICE agents outside her courtroom in April 2025, putting the case back on track for sentencing.” Read more here.

#3 “What the CLEAR Report Means for Regulation of the Legal Profession.” From Chief Justice Megan K. Cavanah in the Michigan Bar Journal: “‘If you can ‘think’ like a lawyer, does that mean you can ‘act’ like a lawyer?’ That’s the question asked and answered in a report published last year by the Committee on Legal Education and Admissions Reform (the CLEAR report). Endorsed by the Conference of Chief Justices (CCJ) and the Conference of State Court Administrators (COSCA), the report reflects a wide-ranging stakeholder analysis that included feedback from thousands of judges, attorneys, and law students. What did CLEAR find?” Read more here.

#4 “Judge Recuses Herself in Georgia Voter Rolls Case.” From Politico: “An embattled federal judge in Atlanta has agreed to the Justice Department’s request that she step aside from a federal lawsuit seeking access to Georgia’s voter rolls. In an order dated Monday and released Tuesday, U.S. District Judge Eleanor Ross said she was recusing herself from the case due to her attendance at a political event in 2024 for Fulton County District Attorney Fani Willis, who brought a criminal case against President Donald Trump for tampering with the 2020 presidential election.” Read more here.

#5 “Texas Lawyer Beats Suspension Over Opponent No-Contact Rule.” From Bloomberg Law: “Texas disciplinary rules don’t expressly bar an attorney representing himself from contacting an opposing party he knows to be represented by counsel, the state supreme court ruled Friday. Suggesting perhaps an administrative change is coming to prohibit such communications, the justices said the case before them wasn’t the appropriate place to make that change. ‘We do not revise our rules by opinion,’ Justice Debra Lehrmann wrote for a nearly unanimous court. The court’s interpretation of the no-contact rule wipes out a five-year suspension a trial court had imposed against licensed attorney William Ruth, which an appeals court had upheld.” Read more here.

#6 “University of Texas Law Alters AI Policy to Stop Skills Loss.” From Bloomberg Law: “The University of Texas School of Law’s dean is calling for a new approach with generative artificial intelligence policies to counter the risk that the technology will erode the learning of essential skills. Faculty should emphasize Socratic-style questioning in teaching, Dean Robert ‘Bobby’ Chesney said in a Tuesday memo to staff. They should view classroom time as ‘the sole context in which a professor can be certain’ a student is learning without AI.” Read more here.

#7 “SCOTUSblog Founder Goldstein Denied Acquittal Or Retrial.” From Law360: “A Maryland federal judge on Tuesday denied SCOTUSblog founder Tom Goldstein’s bid for an acquittal or new trial, rejecting his claims that issues with jury instructions and excluded evidence warranted a do-over in his tax evasion and mortgage fraud case.” Read more here.

#8 “Supreme Court Won’t Hear Bid By Suspended Judge, 98, to Keep Her Job.” From USA Today: “The U.S. Supreme Court declined on June 15 to hear a bid by the nation’s oldest federal judge, 98-year-old Pauline Newman, to overturn her suspension from duties in 2023 during an investigation into her fitness to serve.” Read more here.

#9 “AI Competence is No Longer Optional, Judges Tell Bar Convention.” From The Florida Bar News: “Florida lawyers have a duty to familiarize themselves with AI, a technology that is already transforming the practice of law and the rules of court procedure, veteran judges warned a symposium June 17 at The Florida Bar Convention in Orlando. ‘We all have a basic requirement to competency and something everybody is going to have to do on this front is at least understand the broad concepts of how these tools work,’ said U.S. Magistrate Judge Nicholas Mizell, who presides in Ft. Myers in the Middle District of Florida.” Read more here.

#10 “Ex-Trump Lawyer Wants Reprimanded Judge’s Jan. 6 Ruling Vacated.” From Bloomberg Law: “Former Trump White House lawyer Stefan Passantino asked a censured federal trial judge in Atlanta to vacate her previous ruling against him in a lawsuit challenging the US House Jan. 6 Committee, citing the judge’s close ties to Democratic Fulton County District Attorney Fani Willis. Judge Eleanor Ross of the US District Court for the Northern District of Georgia has already admitted that she attended a campaign victory party for Willis and recused herself from another political case involving the US Justice Department’s attempt to obtain Georgia voter rolls, Passantino’s Thursday motion said.” Read more here.
